key_insight: SevenNet-Polar is a multitask equivariant GNN that predicts Born effective charges alongside energy, forces, and stress with near-DFT accuracy, enabling fast charge-aware materials simulations.
engineering_relevance: It lowers the computational barrier for electric-field and polarization-aware modeling, making large-scale atomistic simulations of functional oxides and perovskites practical on GPUs.
method: Built on the SevenNet equivariant graph neural network architecture.
Trained BEC-only and all-in-one multitask models for energy, forces, stress, and Born effective charges.
Accelerated inference with FlashTP for large-scale simulations on single and multi-GPU systems.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports BEC RMSE as low as 0.0043 e for BEC-only models and 0.0029 e in multitask models, with energy/force/stress errors of 1.0 meV/atom, 12 meV/angstrom, and 0.05 GPa, respectively.
watch_item: BEC scaling is less favorable than energy, force, and stress, so accuracy may degrade more noticeably when extrapolating to unseen structural environments or highly off-distribution configurations.
paper_title: SevenNet-Polar for MultiTask Prediction of Energy, Forces, Stress, and Born Effective Charges: Development and Application to ZrO$_2$, Li$_3$PO$_4$, and Perovskites
